name: Suspicious PSExec Module Usage Detected
notes: This rule triggers when a usage of the PSExec module is detected.
bb_references: BB:BehaviorDefinition: Programming Environment Started with a Privileged Account
BB:BehaviorDefinition: Programming Environment Started with a Privileged Account match at least 4 times with the same Destination IP in 5 minutes
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Successful Registry Modification, Audit.Object Access Success, System.Process Creation Success, System.Service Installed

when Service Configured to Use Powershell, Encoded Command Malicious Usage in a Programming Environment, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Administrative Share Accessed, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Programming Environment Started with a Privileged Account match at least 4 times with the same Destination IP in 5 minutes
thresholds: when Service Configured to Use Powershell, Encoded Command Malicious Usage in a Programming Environment, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Administrative Share Accessed, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Programming Environment Started with a Privileged Account match at least 4 times with the same Destination IP in 5 minutes
TD_list: APPLY Suspicious PSExec Module Usage Detected on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Successful Registry Modification, Audit.Object Access Success, System.Process Creation Success, System.Service Installed

AND when Service Configured to Use Powershell, Encoded Command Malicious Usage in a Programming Environment, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Administrative Share Accessed, BB:BehaviorDefinition: Programming Environment Started with a Privileged Account match at least 4 times with the same Destination IP in 5 minutes
RED_list: Event name: Suspicious PSExec Module Usage Detected
Event description: Detects when a usage of the PSExec module is observed.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
